title: PS-70018 POM updates for Drawing detail overlay. Validating UDF field and empty Sharing Log.
body: Updates `UserCanEditCustomFieldsInDrawingPropertiesPanel` browser test to use the detail overlay instead of the info panel for additional info fields. It also adds a basic sharing log visibility check.
author: JustineK-Trimble

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR updates the Drawings parallel browser test + page objects to interact with the new Drawing detail overlay (instead of the legacy info panel tabs) when validating UDF fields, and adds a basic “Sharing Log is visible and empty” check.

**Changes:**
- Updated `UserCanEditCustomFieldsInDrawingPropertiesPanel` to open the “More info” detail overlay and validate UDF presence/value there.
- Added a Sharing Log tab visibility + empty state text assertion to the same test.
- Extended the `DrawingFDEF` page object model to expose a `DetailOverlay` (“InfoOverlay”) and a “More info” button.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelBrowserTests/Records/Drawing/DrawingPropertiesPanel.cs | Switches the UDF edit/verify flow to the detail overlay and asserts Sharing Log empty-state behavior. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelBrowserTests/_Pages/Pages/DrawingFDEF.cs | Adds POM accessors for the detail overlay and the “More info” button; introduces overlay locators for UDF + Sharing Log. |
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

reviews.body:
I haven't looksd in great detail yet, but I think we should have 2 more scenarios:
1. User without permission cannot see Sharing log tab, but can see Additional info
2. User without permission and when there's no UDF cannot see More info link at all
reviews.author: alexpaller

review_comments.body: ChangeFeaturePermission returns the previous permission level; setting this to IsAllowed without capturing the return value makes it impossible to restore the original state later (and the test currently hard-resets to IsDenied). Capture the previous value so the test can restore whatever was configured before it ran.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The cleanup at the end of the test currently forces CanViewSharingHistory to IsDenied, which may not match the previous configured value for this user group. Restore the previously captured permission level instead.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Feature permissions are shared test state; if any assertion fails before the end of the method, the permission will remain modified and can cascade failures into later tests. Wrap the test body in a try/finally (or restore in CustomTestCleanupAsync) so the original permission is always restored (see docs/ai/INTEGRATION_TEST_CONVENTIONS.md §9).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The drawing detail overlay root element is always present in the DOM and is shown/hidden via the `[hidden]` attribute (see drawing-detail-overlay.component.html). Using `.Exists()` against `.drawing-detail-overlay` can report true even when the overlay is not actually visible. Scope the overlay to `:not([hidden])` (or equivalent XPath `not(@hidden)`) so IsShown() and child locators only succeed when the overlay is open.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body:
`_originalSharingHistoryPermission` is assigned but never used. In this test project `TreatWarningsAsErrors` is enabled (`Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelBrowserTests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelBrowserTests.csproj:11`), so this will fail the build as an unused-variable warning.

Either remove the assignment (if cleanup is the intended reset path), or actually use the returned value to restore the prior permission in `finally`/`CustomTestCleanupAsync`.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Cleanup restores CanViewSharingHistory to PermissionLevel.IsDenied based on TestContext.TestName. That assumes the default permission is always denied and also risks leaking the permission change if the test is renamed or if other tests in this class later toggle the same permission. Prefer tracking the previous permission level when you change it, and restoring it unconditionally in cleanup when it was modified.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This permission toggle isn’t formatted consistently with the surrounding code, and it also discards the previous permission level. Capture the previous value so cleanup can restore it, and format the call like the other multi-arg invocations in this file.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `SharingLogEmptyMessage.Exists();` is a no-op (its return value is ignored). Turning this into an assertion gives a clearer failure mode if the sharing log never finishes loading or the empty state doesn’t render. Also, `Be("…")` is the clearer assertion for an exact string match than `BeEquivalentTo`.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: See if the `ModusButton` POM would work here instead since we have that control available.
review_comments.author: henrykaus

review_comments.body: For new private variables, we follow the newer standard of just `_`, so in this case, that would be `_OverlayScope`
review_comments.author: henrykaus

review_comments.body: This likely should be its own test or part of its own test specific to the sharing log. We probably don't need to do extra testing on the sharing log in this UDF-specific test. When this is the case, you could just have generic initialize and cleanups relating to the sharing log permission
review_comments.author: henrykaus

review_comments.body: You only need the second line here, `_FDEF.InfoOverlay.AdditionalInfoTab.Exists()` will wait 30 seconds or until the element exists before doing the assertion
review_comments.author: henrykaus

review_comments.body: Is that POM on a feature branch? I only see ModusCheckbox and ModusSearch in main.
review_comments.author: JustineK-Trimble

review_comments.body: Yeah, I think you're right. This was meant to just fix the failing test, but got conflated. Going to make a follow-up ticket for Sharing Log tests including the ones mentioned by @alexpaller above.
review_comments.author: JustineK-Trimble

review_comments.body: I got the naming wrong, the POM is just called `Button` in `Button.cs`
review_comments.author: henrykaus
